text: Second-hand clothing (mitumba) trading is one of Uganda's largest informal sectors. Bales of imported used clothing are sold in Owino/St. Balikuddembe Market, Kampala. Startup: 1 bale costs UGX 200,000-800,000 depending on quality grade and type (Grade A: premium brands UGX 600K+, Grade B: mixed UGX 300-500K, Grade C: bulk UGX 200-300K). Each bale contains 50-200 pieces. Sell individually at 3-10x cost per piece. Example: buy bale of jeans (100 pieces) at UGX 500,000 = UGX 5,000/piece, sell at UGX 15,000-25,000 each. Revenue per bale: UGX 1,500,000-2,500,000. Profit margin: 40-70%. Best locations: market stalls, roadside displays, Facebook/WhatsApp selling. Key success: develop an eye for quality — knowing which brands and sizes sell fast in your area. Sort bales immediately: separate premium pieces for retail, bulk items for wholesale.
